question: why does overexpression of rtks lead to cancer development
reference: a hyper-active kinase, that confers an aberrant, ligand-independent, non-regulated growth stimulus to the cancer cells
answer: Overexpression of RTKs (receptor tyrosine kinases) can lead to cancer development due to their role in regulating cellular processes such as proliferation, differentiation, and survival. Uncontrolled activation of RTKs can promote unchecked cell growth and division, leading to the formation of tumors. Additionally, RTKs can also modulate the activity of other signaling pathways involved in cancer development, such as the PI3K/Akt and MAPK pathways

question: why does overexpression of rtks lead to cancer development
reference: a hyper-active kinase, that confers an aberrant, ligand-independent, non-regulated growth stimulus to the cancer cells
answer: Overexpression of RTKs (receptor tyrosine kinases) can lead to cancer development due to their role in regulating cellular processes such as proliferation, differentiation, and survival. Uncontrolled activation of RTKs can promote unchecked cell growth and division, leading to the formation of tumors. Additionally, RTKs can also modulate the activity of other signaling pathways involved in cancer development, such as the PI3K/Akt and MAPK pathways
